stem: A decision-maker must choose among 3 energy source options. The probability of favorable regional climate data is uncertain, estimated between 38% and 49%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: solar: $143K in good regional climate data, $82K in bad regional climate data. wind: $105K in good regional climate data, $28K in bad regional climate data. natural gas: $113K in good regional climate data, $31K in bad regional climate data. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.43: best option is solar with EV=$108.2K. solar is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [105.2, 111.9].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
solar is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
